Corn always has an even number of rows on each cob.
The King of Hearts is the only king in a standard deck of cards without a mustache.
A group of stingrays is called a fever.
Dolphins are so smart that the U.S. Navy has used them to detect underwater mines.
The original name for the search engine Google was Backrub.
Apples are more effective at waking you up in the morning than coffee.
A group of ladybugs is called a loveliness.
The average person's left hand does 56% of the typing on a QWERTY keyboard.
Astronauts report that space smells like seared steak and hot metal.
More people are killed by vending machines each year than by sharks.
It takes about 63,000 trees to make the newsprint for one Sunday edition of the New York Times.
The longest word in English with all its letters in alphabetical order is 'almost.'
A jiffy bag was named after the unit of time — the idea being you could post something in a jiffy.
Your body replaces about 330 billion cells per day — that's about 3.8 million cells per second.
The youngest pope was 18 years old when he took office in the year 1032.
Camels have three eyelids to protect their eyes from blowing sand in the desert.
A group of flamingos is a flamboyance, but a group of 12 or more cows is called a flink.
The longest word typed with only the left hand on a standard keyboard is 'stewardesses.'
The smell of freshly baked bread has been shown to make people friendlier and more willing to help strangers.
About 85% of plant life is found in the ocean.
A newborn kangaroo is about 1 inch long — the size of a lima bean.
The Earth's rotation is gradually slowing — days were shorter millions of years ago.
Mosquitoes have 47 teeth.
The word 'nerd' was first coined by Dr. Seuss in the book 'If I Ran the Zoo' in 1950.
A crocodile's brain is about the size of a walnut.
The shortest English word containing all five vowels is 'eunoia,' meaning beautiful thinking.

An ant can survive being submerged in water for up to 24 hours.
The average raindrop falls at a speed of roughly 14 miles per hour.
A giraffe's heart is about 2 feet long and weighs roughly 25 pounds.
The world's largest ball of twine weighs over 17,000 pounds and is in Kansas.
A cubic mile of ordinary fog contains less than a gallon of water.
The first person convicted of speeding was going 8 mph in a 2 mph zone.

The average iceberg weighs about 20 million tons.
A group of ferrets is called a business.
There is a species of shrimp that can snap its claw so fast it creates a flash of light and a sound louder than a gunshot.
The first email was sent in 1971 by Ray Tomlinson to himself.
Dogs can understand up to 250 words and gestures.
The world's smallest frog is about the size of a housefly.
Honey is essentially dehydrated nectar — bees fan it with their wings to evaporate excess moisture.
The Sun loses about 4 million tons of mass per second through nuclear fusion.
A frog completely sheds its skin about once a week, and usually eats it.
There are more synaptic connections in the human brain than stars in the Milky Way.
The speed of electricity through a copper wire is about two-thirds the speed of light.
A group of larks is called an exaltation.
The first webcam was used at Cambridge University to monitor a coffee pot.
An eagle can spot a rabbit from over a mile away.
The average person produces about 10,000 gallons of saliva in their lifetime.
The Earth's inner core is roughly the size of the Moon.
A baby puffin is called a puffling.
One year on Neptune is 165 Earth years — Neptune completed its first full orbit since discovery in 2011.
A group of baboons is called a congress.
The letter J is the last letter to be added to the English alphabet.
The average human body generates enough electricity to power a small flashlight.
Watermelons are 92% water, making them one of the most hydrating foods.
The world's oldest piece of chewing gum is 9,000 years old, made from birch bark tar.
The Earth is hit by lightning about 100 times every second.
A flock of crows is called a murder, and they hold 'funerals' by gathering around dead members.
The hottest temperature ever recorded on Earth was 134°F (56.7°C) in Death Valley, California.
Bees can fly higher than Mount Everest.
The smallest bones in the human body are the ossicles in the middle ear.
There are about 1,500 active volcanoes on Earth, and about 50 to 70 erupt each year.
A colony of 40,000 ants is collectively as intelligent as a human being.
The longest English word with all the letters in reverse alphabetical order is 'spoonfed.'
Reindeer eyes change color from gold in summer to blue in winter to help them see in low light.
A violin contains about 70 separate pieces of wood assembled together.
The average adult heart beats about 2.5 billion times in a 75-year lifespan.
One in every 200 people alive today is a direct descendant of Genghis Khan.
An eagle's eye has a resolution roughly eight times sharper than a human's.
There are more possible shuffles of a deck of cards than seconds since the Big Bang.
Pistol shrimp create underwater bubbles that are as hot as the Sun's surface for a fraction of a second.
The average cloud travels at about 30 mph.
A queen bee can lay up to 2,000 eggs in a single day.
The word 'piano' is short for 'pianoforte,' meaning soft-loud in Italian.
The longest mountain range on Earth is the mid-ocean ridge system, stretching over 40,000 miles underwater.
There are enough diamonds on Jupiter and Saturn to make it rain gems.
Some lobsters are naturally blue — about 1 in 2 million lobsters are born with this coloration.
A flea can accelerate 50 times faster than a space shuttle during its jump.
A cubic foot of gold weighs over 1,200 pounds.
Crows can recognize individual human faces and pass that knowledge to other crows.
Water makes up about 60% of an adult human body.
An average ear of corn has about 800 kernels arranged in 16 rows.
The shortest scheduled airline flight is between Westray and Papa Westray in Scotland — about 90 seconds.
A group of foxes is called a skulk.
The total weight of all bacteria on Earth exceeds the total weight of all plants and animals combined.
The fastest bird in level flight is the common swift, reaching speeds of about 69 mph.
The human eye can detect a candle flame from about 1.7 miles away on a clear, dark night.
A group of iguanas is called a mess.
Every atom in your body is billions of years old — hydrogen atoms formed shortly after the Big Bang.
The Pacific Ocean at its widest point is wider than the Moon.
A dog's nose print is unique, similar to a human fingerprint.
The world's largest living flower, Rafflesia, smells like rotting meat to attract pollinating flies.
Octopuses can change color in less than a second to match their surroundings.
The average smartphone today has more computing power than the computers used for the Apollo Moon missions.
The tallest waterfall in the world is Angel Falls in Venezuela at 3,212 feet.
An ant can carry 10 to 50 times its own body weight.
The largest hailstone ever recorded in the U.S. weighed nearly 2 pounds.
A photon traveling from the Andromeda galaxy takes 2.5 million years to reach Earth.
The longest word in the Oxford English Dictionary is 'pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis.'
A chameleon's tongue can extend to twice its body length in a fraction of a second.
Your small intestine is about 20 feet long, while your large intestine is about 5 feet long.
A group of giraffes is called a tower.
The world's largest living organism by area is a honey fungus in Oregon spanning over 2,385 acres.
A lightning bolt is only about as wide as a thumb, despite appearing much larger.
The shortest war between two countries was between Britain and Zanzibar, lasting under 45 minutes.
A teaspoon of honey represents the life's work of 12 bees.
The Hubble Space Telescope can see objects 13.4 billion light-years away.
The Greenland shark can live over 400 years, making it the longest-lived vertebrate.
A full moon is nine times brighter than a half moon.
There are approximately 7,000 languages spoken in the world today.
The cornea is the only part of the human body that has no blood supply — it gets oxygen directly from the air.
Honey never spoils — archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood due to a copper-based molecule called hemocyanin.
A group of flamingos is called a "flamboyance."
The shortest war in history lasted 38 minutes, between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896.
Bananas are naturally slightly radioactive because they contain potassium-40.
The inventor of the Pringles can, Fredric Baur, was buried in one after he passed away in 2008.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping so they don't drift apart from each other.
